Class TermBuilder

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.TermBuilder
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractAuxiliaryContractImpl.Combinator, AbstractAuxiliaryContractImpl.Creator, AuxiliaryContract.VariablesCreator, AuxiliaryContractBuilders.ConditionsAndClausesBuilder, AuxiliaryContractBuilders.UpdatesBuilder, BlockInfFlowUnfoldTacletBuilder, InfFlowBlockContractTacletBuilder, InfFlowLoopInvariantTacletBuilder, InfFlowMethodContractTacletBuilder, InfFlowProgVarRenamer, LoopInfFlowUnfoldTacletBuilder, MethodInfFlowUnfoldTacletBuilder
public class TermBuilder extends Object

Use this class if you intend to build complex terms by hand. It is more convenient than the @link{TermFactory} class.

Attention: some methods of this class try to simplify some terms. So if you want to be sure that the term looks exactly as you built it, you will have to use the TermFactory.

    addLabel(JTerm term, OriginTermLabel.Origin origin)
    add origin information to the term nothing will be done if no origin term label factory is present

    addLabel(JTerm term, TermLabel label)
    Adds a label to a term, removing any existing labels of the same type.
    JTerm
    addLabel(JTerm term, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels)
    Adds labels to a term, removing any existing labels of the same type.
    JTerm
    addLabelToAllSubs(JTerm term, OriginTermLabel.Origin origin)
    add origin information to the term and all its sub terms nothing will be done if no origin term label factory is present
    JTerm
    addLabelToAllSubs(JTerm term, TermLabel label)
    Applies the label to the term and almost every (direct or indirect) sub-term recursively.
    JTerm

    Applies the labels to the term and almost every (direct or indirect) sub-term recursively.

    Similar behavior as or(Iterable) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.

    • tf

      public TermFactory tf()

    • parseTerm

      public JTerm parseTerm(String s) throws ParserException
      Parses the given string that represents the term (or createTerm) using the service's namespaces.
      Parameters:
      s - the String to parse
      Throws:
      ParserException

    • parseTerm

      public JTerm parseTerm(String s, NamespaceSet namespaces) throws ParserException
      Parses the given string that represents the term (or createTerm) using the provided namespaces.
      Parameters:
      s - the String to parse
      namespaces - the namespaces used for name lookup.
      Throws:
      ParserException - if the given String cannot be parsed

    • shortBaseName

      public String shortBaseName(Sort s)

    • newName

      public String newName(String baseName)
      Returns an available name constructed by affixing a counter to the passed base name.

      This method looks up the global NamespaceSet to check whether the Names is free. This can be problematic, since Namespaces are now local to goals. Use newName(String, NamespaceSet) to make sure that you have all the Names you need available.

      Parameters:
      baseName - The base name (prefix) for the name to generate.
      Returns:
      An available name constructed by affixing a counter to the passed base name, or some available free name (please consult comment above).
      See Also:
      • newName(String, NamespaceSet)

    • newName

      public String newName(String baseName, NamespaceSet localNamespace)
      Returns an available name constructed by affixing a counter to the passed base name.

      Warning (DS): This method ignores the baseName if there are free name proposals. This can, for instance, cause troubles in loading proofs containing rule apps with more than one introduced (and saved) new name. In this case, the order of new names in the saved proof file matters (the first unused name is returned, regardless of the baseName).

      Parameters:
      baseName - The base name (prefix) for the name to generate.
      localNamespace - The local NamespaceSet to check.
      Returns:
      An available name constructed by affixing a counter to the passed base name, or some available free name (please consult comment above).

    • newName

      public String newName(Sort sort)
      Returns an available name constructed by affixing a counter to a self- chosen base name for the passed sort.

    • selfVar

      public LocationVariable selfVar(KeYJavaType kjt, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a program variable for "self". Take care to register it in the namespaces!

    • selfVar

      public LocationVariable selfVar(KeYJavaType kjt, boolean makeNameUnique, String postfix)
      Creates a program variable for "self". Take care to register it in the namespaces!

      public ImmutableList<LocationVariable> paramVars(IObserverFunction obs, boolean makeNamesUnique)
      Creates program variables for the parameters. Take care to register them in the namespaces!

      Creates program variables for the parameters. Take care to register them in the namespaces!

    • resultVar

      public LocationVariable resultVar(IProgramMethod pm, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a program variable for the result. Take care to register it in the namespaces.

    • resultVar

      public LocationVariable resultVar(String name, IProgramMethod pm, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a program variable for the result with passed name. Take care to register it in the namespaces.

    • excVar

      public LocationVariable excVar(IProgramMethod pm, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a program variable for the thrown exception. Take care to register it in the namespaces.

    • excVar

      public LocationVariable excVar(String name, IProgramMethod pm, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a program variable for the thrown exception. Take care to register it in the namespaces.

    • heapAtPreVar

      public LocationVariable heapAtPreVar(String baseName, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a program variable for the atPre heap. Take care to register it in the namespaces.

    • atPreVar

      public LocationVariable atPreVar(String baseName, Sort sort, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a location variable for prestate variables. Take care to register it in the namespaces.
      Parameters:
      baseName - the base name to use
      sort - the sort of the variable
      makeNameUnique - whether to change the base name to be unique
      Returns:
      a location variable for the given name and type

    • atPreVar

      public LocationVariable atPreVar(String baseName, KeYJavaType kjt, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a location variable for prestate variables. Take care to register it in the namespaces.
      Parameters:
      baseName - the base name to use
      kjt - the type of the variable
      makeNameUnique - whether to change the base name to be unique
      Returns:
      a location variable for the given name and type

    • locationVariable

      public LocationVariable locationVariable(String baseName, Sort sort, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a location variable for example for prestate variables. Take care to register it in the namespaces.
      Parameters:
      baseName - the base name to use
      sort - the sort of the variable
      makeNameUnique - whether to change the base name to be unique
      Returns:
      a location variable for the given name and type

    • locationVariable

      public LocationVariable locationVariable(String baseName, KeYJavaType kjt, boolean makeNameUnique)
      Creates a location variable for example for prestate variables. Take care to register it in the namespaces.
      Parameters:
      baseName - the base name to use
      kjt - the type of the variable
      makeNameUnique - whether to change the base name to be unique
      Returns:
      a location variable for the given name and type

    • zero

    • one

      public JTerm one()

    • zTerm

      public JTerm zTerm(String numberString)
      Get term for an integer literal.
      Parameters:
      numberString - String representing an integer with radix 10, may be negative
      Returns:
      Term in Z-Notation representing the given number
      Throws:
      NumberFormatException - if numberString is not a number

    • zTerm

      public JTerm zTerm(long number)
      Get term for an integer literal.
      Parameters:
      number - an integer
      Returns:
      Term in Z-Notation representing the given number

    • cTerm

      public JTerm cTerm(String numberString)
      Parameters:
      numberString - String containing the value of the char as a decimal number
      Returns:
      Term in C-Notation representing the given char
      Throws:
      NumberFormatException - if numberString is not a number

    • fpTerm

      public JTerm fpTerm(float value)
      Create a floating point literal value from a float value.
      Parameters:
      value - any float value (even NaN)
      Returns:
      a term representing the value

    • dfpTerm

      public JTerm dfpTerm(double value)
      Create a double floating point literal value from a double value.
      Parameters:
      value - any double value (even NaN)
      Returns:
      a term representing the value

      public JTerm add(JTerm t1, JTerm t2)

    • inLong

      public JTerm inLong(JTerm var)

    • index

      public JTerm index()

    • strictlyNothing

      public JTerm strictlyNothing()
      This value is only used as a marker for "\strictly_nothing" in JML. It may return any term. Preferably of type LocSet, but this is not necessary.
      Returns:
      an arbitrary but fixed term.

    • empty

      public JTerm empty()

      public JTerm allLocs()

    • NULL

      public JTerm NULL()

    • deepNonNull

      public JTerm deepNonNull(JTerm o, JTerm d)
      The "deep non null" predicate arising from JML non_null types. Deep non null means that it is recursively defined for arrays. See bug #1392.

      public JTerm wellFormed(JTerm heap)

    • wellFormed

      public JTerm wellFormed(LocationVariable heap)

      public JTerm permissionsFor(JTerm permHeap, JTerm regularHeap)

      public JTerm permissionsFor(LocationVariable permHeap, LocationVariable regularHeap)

      public JTerm inv(JTerm[] h, JTerm o)

    • inv

      public JTerm inv(JTerm o)

      public JTerm staticInv(JTerm[] h, KeYJavaType t)

    • staticInv

      public JTerm staticInv(KeYJavaType t)

      public JTerm invFree(JTerm[] h, JTerm o)

    • invFree

      public JTerm invFree(JTerm o)

      public JTerm staticInvFree(JTerm[] h, KeYJavaType t)

      public JTerm staticInvFree(KeYJavaType t)

    • select

      public JTerm select(Sort asSort, JTerm h, JTerm o, JTerm f)

    • select

      public JTerm select(Sort asSort, JTerm h, JTerm o, LocationVariable field)
      Get the select expression for a location variabe representing the field.

    • dot

      public JTerm dot(Sort asSort, JTerm o, JTerm f)

    • getBaseHeap

      public JTerm getBaseHeap()

    • dot

      public JTerm dot(Sort asSort, JTerm o, Function f)

    • dot

      public JTerm dot(Sort asSort, JTerm o, LocationVariable field)

    • staticDot

      public JTerm staticDot(Sort asSort, JTerm f)

    • staticDot

      public JTerm staticDot(Sort asSort, Function f)

    • finalDot

      public JTerm finalDot(Sort sort, JTerm o, Function f)
      Get a term for accessing a final field. This can be used for ordinary fields and model fields. The results are quite different!
      Parameters:
      sort - the sort of the result.
      o - the object to access
      f - the field to access
      Returns:
      the term representing the access "o.f"
      See Also:
      • for accessing final Java or ghost fields
      • for accessing final model fields

    • staticFinalDot

      public JTerm staticFinalDot(Sort sort, Function f)
      Get a term for accessing a static final field. This can be used for ordinary fields.
      Parameters:
      sort - the sort of the result.
      f - the field to access
      Returns:
      the term representing the static access "C.f"
      See Also:
      • for accessing final Java or ghost fields
      • for accessing final model fields

    • finalDot

      public JTerm finalDot(Sort asSort, JTerm o, JTerm f)
      Final fields can be treated differently outside the heap. This methods creates a heap-independent read access to final field.
      Parameters:
      asSort - the sort of the result.
      o - the object to access
      f - the field to access
      Returns:
      the term representing the access "o.f"

    • arr

      public JTerm arr(JTerm idx)

    • addLabelToAllSubs

      public JTerm addLabelToAllSubs(JTerm term, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels)
      Applies the labels to the term and almost every (direct or indirect) sub-term recursively.

      The labels are not added to heap variables.

      Parameters:
      term - term to label.
      labels - the labels to apply.
      Returns:
      a labeled term.

    • addLabelToAllSubs

      public JTerm addLabelToAllSubs(JTerm term, TermLabel label)
      Applies the label to the term and almost every (direct or indirect) sub-term recursively.

      The label is not added to heap variables.

      Parameters:
      term - term to label.
      label - the label to apply.
      Returns:
      a labeled term.

    • addLabel

      public JTerm addLabel(JTerm term, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels)
      Adds labels to a term, removing any existing labels of the same type.
      Parameters:
      term - the term.
      labels - the labels to add.
      Returns:
      the term with the labels added.

    • addLabel

      public JTerm addLabel(JTerm term, TermLabel label)
      Adds a label to a term, removing any existing labels of the same type.
      Parameters:
      term - the term.
      label - the label to add.
      Returns:
      the term with the label added.

    • label

      public JTerm label(JTerm term, ImmutableArray<TermLabel> labels)
      Applies labels to a term, removing any existing labels.
      Parameters:
      term - the term.
      labels - the labels to apply.
      Returns:
      the modified term.

    • label

      public JTerm label(JTerm term, TermLabel label)
      Applies a label to a term, removing any existing labels.
      Parameters:
      term - the term.
      label - the label to apply.
      Returns:
      the modified term.

    • frame

      public JTerm frame(JTerm heapTerm, Map<JTerm,JTerm> normalToAtPre, JTerm modifiable)

    • frameStrictlyEmpty

      public JTerm frameStrictlyEmpty(JTerm heapTerm, Map<JTerm,JTerm> normalToAtPre)
      Returns the framing condition that the resulting heap is identical (i.e. has the same value in all locations) to the before-heap.
      See Also:
      • frame(JTerm, Map, JTerm)

    • anonUpd

      public JTerm anonUpd(LocationVariable heap, JTerm modifiable, JTerm anonHeap)

    • seqLen

      public JTerm seqLen(JTerm s)

      public JTerm indexOf(JTerm s, JTerm x)
      Function representing the least index of an element x in a sequence s (or underspecified)

      public JTerm seqEmpty()

      public JTerm seqSingleton(JTerm x)

      public JTerm seq(JTerm... terms)

    • seq

      public JTerm seq(ImmutableList<JTerm> terms)

    • seqUpd

      public JTerm seqUpd(JTerm seq, JTerm idx, JTerm value)

    • seqReverse

      public JTerm seqReverse(JTerm s)

    • replace

      public JTerm replace(JTerm term, PosInTerm pos, JTerm replacement)
      Replaces a child term by another one.
      Parameters:
      term - the term in which to perform the replacement.
      pos - the position at which to perform the replacement.
      replacement - the replacement term.
      Returns:
      term, with the child at pos replaced by replacement.

    • unionToSet

      public ImmutableSet<JTerm> unionToSet(JTerm s)

    • goBelowUpdates

      public static JTerm goBelowUpdates(JTerm term)
      Removes leading updates from the passed term.

    • goBelowUpdates2

      public static Pair<ImmutableList<JTerm>, JTerm> goBelowUpdates2(JTerm term)
      Removes leading updates from the passed term.

    • seqDef

      public JTerm seqDef(QuantifiableVariable qv, JTerm a, JTerm b, JTerm t)

    • values

      public JTerm values()

    • getSorts

      public ImmutableList<Sort> getSorts(Iterable<JTerm> terms)
      Returns the Sorts of the given JTerms.
      Parameters:
      terms - The given JTerms.
      Returns:
      The JTerm Sorts.

    • impPreserveLabels

      public JTerm impPreserveLabels(JTerm t1, JTerm t2)
      Similar behavior as imp(JTerm, JTerm) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.
      Parameters:
      t1 - The left side.
      t2 - The right side.
      Returns:
      The created JTerm.

    • notPreserveLabels

      public JTerm notPreserveLabels(JTerm t)
      Similar behavior as not(JTerm) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.
      Parameters:
      t - The child JTerm.
      Returns:
      The created JTerm.

    • andPreserveLabels

      public JTerm andPreserveLabels(Iterable<JTerm> subTerms)
      Similar behavior as and(Iterable) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.
      Parameters:
      subTerms - The sub JTerms.
      Returns:
      The created JTerm.

    • andPreserveLabels

      public JTerm andPreserveLabels(JTerm t1, JTerm t2)
      Similar behavior as and(JTerm, JTerm) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.
      Parameters:
      t1 - The left side.
      t2 - The right side.
      Returns:
      The created JTerm.

    • orPreserveLabels

      public JTerm orPreserveLabels(Iterable<JTerm> subTerms)
      Similar behavior as or(Iterable) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.
      Parameters:
      subTerms - The sub JTerms.
      Returns:
      The created JTerm.

    • orPreserveLabels

      public JTerm orPreserveLabels(JTerm t1, JTerm t2)
      Similar behavior as or(JTerm, JTerm) but simplifications are not performed if TermLabels would be lost.
      Parameters:
      t1 - The left side.
      t2 - The right side.
      Returns:
      The created JTerm.

    • fpEq

      public JTerm fpEq(JTerm t1, JTerm t2)
      Floating-point aware equality for floats and double

    • addLabelToAllSubs

      public JTerm addLabelToAllSubs(JTerm term, OriginTermLabel.Origin origin)
      add origin information to the term and all its sub terms nothing will be done if no origin term label factory is present
      Parameters:
      term - the term where to start to add the origin information
      origin - the Origin information
      Returns:
      the labeled term or the same term, if no origin term label factory is present

    • addLabel

      public JTerm addLabel(JTerm term, OriginTermLabel.Origin origin)
      add origin information to the term nothing will be done if no origin term label factory is present
      Parameters:
      term - the term where to add the origin information
      origin - the Origin information
      Returns:
      the labeled term or the same term, if no origin term label factory is present

    • getOriginFactory

      public OriginTermLabelFactory getOriginFactory()
      returns the origin term label factory
      Returns:
      the OriginTermLabelFactory
